title: Burnout
style: vocal-forward, spacious, slow acoustic soul, heavy and tired but beautiful, nylon guitar plus warm cello and soft organ and muted percussion, male vocal weary and soulful close-mic with aching falsetto, studio quality warm soulful mix, 72 BPM, key of Bb, reference Bon Iver weariness, reference Chet Baker melancholy, reference Jon Guerra soul
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Five AM alarm, the slack is lit
Fourteen notifications, none of it
Is what I signed up for back in the day
When building things felt more like play
Andy Johns — seventeen years in the race
Facebook, Twitter, Wealthfront — couldn't slow the pace

[Chorus]
Burnout isn't working too much
It's working on the wrong things
Pouring from a cup that's empty to the touch
Optimizing for a game you don't want to win
And the scoreboard's bright but the lights are wearing thin
Burnout, burnout
When did I stop breathing?

[Verse 2]
Forty-five percent of tech is fried
Feather, brick, then dump truck — can't deny
The signs were there, you thought you'd power through
But the body keeps the score and it knows the truth
It's not the hours, it's the hollow ache
Of building castles for someone else's sake

[Chorus]
Burnout isn't working too much
It's working on the wrong things
Pouring from a cup that's empty to the touch
Optimizing for a game you don't want to win
And the scoreboard's bright but the lights are wearing thin
Burnout, burnout
When did I stop breathing?

[Outro]
You can't pour from an empty cup
But you can set it down
You can set it down

source.title: The real cost of burnout in tech
